post.content: My system design question was to design a heatmap. Pretty much user can input any range of times (in minutes) and I want to be able to see the density of drivers on the map color coded You don\'t have to worry about the actual color coding part (assume you have some UI that will take in the count input and do the coloring for you). I did well on the coding and was told I was rejected on the system design round. Was wondering if anyone can give a good solution to this?

Interesting, I was just given the same problem during Amazon on-site coding for Amazon trucks of course 😁.

The solution I did was to log every truck time ping into a dictionary/map by location.   In this case you would have to split the area into a grid, so each car location gets assigned a grid cell. 

Now, to calculate the number of trucks during the time range, you could do a binary search on start and end times for every cell.   The difference between the time series indices is the density. 

There's an issue with many cars sending too many data points.   So, the granularity of the query or the frequency of the data collection and the cell size would have to be adjusted.

Also if the time range is too long relative  to the cell size,  one truck could be counted several times in different cells.
was it LLD or HLD? How did you approach to this problem?
Sorry for the rejection! I had a pretty shitty Amazon interview myself. Can you give more details about the system? And also the coding round?
